problem: Let $f : \mathbb{C} \to \mathbb{C} $ be defined by $ f(z) = z^2 + iz + 1 $. How many complex numbers $z $ are there such that $ \text{Im}(z) > 0 $ and both the real and the imaginary parts of $f(z)$ are integers with absolute value at most $ 10 $?

classified_questions.question: Can you explain what the function $f(z)$ represents?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we find the complex numbers $z$ for which $\text{Im}(z) > 0$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the quadratic formula and how does it apply to this problem?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we determine the real and imaginary parts of $f(z)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why do we need the absolute value of the real and imaginary parts to be at most $10$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain how to find the imaginary part of a radical of a complex number?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we express the condition $\text{Im}(z) > 0$ in terms of the real and imaginary parts of $z$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the inequality that we need to satisfy in order to have $\text{Im}(z) > 0$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.label: b
